new_classes.label_en: Other Vinegar (not wine vinegar) in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2209 00 91)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Essig (kein Weinessig) in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 L (KN 2209 00 91)
new_classes.definition_en: Vinegar other than wine vinegar, including fermentation vinegar from acetic acid fermentation of ethyl alcohol, beer, fruit juices, or other substrates, and chemical (non-fermentation) vinegar (diluted acetic acid), presented in containers holding 2 litres or less, classified under CN 2209 00 91.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for 22090091 states "Other, in containers holding [≤ 2 L]" and cross-references HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209, part (I) second paragraph (2)(3)(4) and part (II). This covers: (2) fermentation vinegar from ethyl alcohol, (3) fermentation vinegar from beer/fruit/other substrates, (4) spirit vinegar, and (II) non-fermentation (chemical/dilute acetic acid) vinegar — all being non-wine vinegar types in small containers (≤ 2 L). The key discriminating criteria captured are: (a) it is a Vinegar (subclass of eucn:Vinegar), (b) it is NOT wine vinegar (complement of WineVinegar, expressed via the complement restriction), (c) it is presented in containers ≤ 2 L (SmallContainer part restriction). The 'AceticFermentationQuality' referenced in the third restriction is not yet in the TBox — this slightly reduces coverage; that restriction cannot be fully resolved. The distinction from the sibling 22090099 (containers > 2 L) is captured via the SmallContainer restriction. Coverage is partial because the full typology of non-wine vinegar substrates (ethyl alcohol, beer, fruit, chemical acetic acid) is described only narratively in HS explanatory notes and cannot be fully formalised without additional feedstock/process classes. The complement restriction uses a dummy property IRI since OWL complement in this tool schema requires a property_iri field — the intent is owl:disjointWith WineVinegar.
